chief_complaint: A 60-year-old man presented with non-ST elevation myocardial infarction.
history: The patient underwent emergency revascularization for a severely calcified lesion in the left circumflex artery. Despite initial stent implantation, intravascular ultrasound demonstrated stent underexpansion.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: ECG showed signs of non-ST elevation myocardial infarction. Blood tests and imaging results were not provided.
correct_diagnosis: Stent underexpansion in a severely calcified lesion
differentials: Stent underexpansion in a severely calcified lesion
Myocardial infarction due to coronary artery disease
correct_ranking: Stent underexpansion in a severely calcified lesion
Myocardial infarction due to coronary artery disease
